---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "subset_obsp"
namespace: "Filter"
description: "Create a subset of an .obsp field in a mudata file, by filtering the columns based on the values of an .obs column."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `subset_obsp`  
Namespace: `filter`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/filter/subset_obsp)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/filter/subset_obsp)

:::

The resulting subset is moved to an .obsm slot


## Argument groups

### Arguments

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                  |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"` |


### Input

|Name                |Description                                           |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`           |Input h5mu file                                       |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"` |
|`--modality`        |Which modality from the input MuData file to process. |`string`, default: `"rna"`                |
|`--input_obsp_key`  |The .obsp field to be filtered.                       |`string`, required                        |
|`--input_obs_key`   |The .obs column to filter on.                         |`string`, required                        |
|`--input_obs_value` |The value to filter on in the .obs column.            |`string`, required                        |


### Output

|Name                |Description                           |Attributes                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`          |Output h5mu file.                     |`file`, example: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--output_obsm_key` |The .obsm key to store the subset in. |`string`, required               |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (author, maintainer)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/filter/subset_obsp/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/filter/subset_obsp).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/filter/subset_obsp/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
